revision.text:
[[en:Joenkahofun]][[ja:上円下方墳]] ---- {{Short description|Type of ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}} [[File:Musashi-Fuchu Kumano-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|[[Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun]]]] {{nihongo|{{ill|Joenkahofun|lt=Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} is a kind of [[Kofun]]..{{Cite web |title=Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties |url=https://www.nabunken.go.jp/english/monograph/72.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.nabunken.go.jp}}{{Cite web |title=Kofun Culture |url=http://www.t-net.ne.jp/~keally/kofun.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.t-net.ne.jp}}{{Cite web |last=Hays |first=Jeffrey |title=ASUKA, FUJIWARA AND ASUKA-ERA CITIES AND TOMBS {{!}} Facts and Details |url=https://factsanddetails.com/japan/cat16/sub106/entry-5299.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=factsanddetails.com |language=en}} Such kofun have a round dome top with a square bottom.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}} {{in lang|ja}} This is a quite rare type of Kofun in Japan. They are associated with the [[:en:Asuka Period|Asuka Period]], [[Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun]] in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|qid=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}} in Tokyo,{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}} {{in lang|ja}} and [[Miyazuka Kofun]] are two notable examples. ==Overview== [[File:Ishi-no-Karato Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|qid=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|1=Ishinokarato Kofun}}
（{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}・{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kizugawa, Kyoto|en|Kizugawa, Kyoto|qid=Q990352|lt=Kizugawa City|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}）}}]] '''Jōen Kahōfun''' (上円下方墳) refers to a unique type of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) built in the [[Japanese archipelago]] in ancient times and, in modern times, as a form of {{ill|imperial|en|imperial|qid=Q226342|lt=imperial|1=Imperial}} mausoleum. == Overview == This kofun type consists of two tiers: a square lower tier and a circular upper tier. It is an extremely rare form, with only six confirmed examples. The first confirmed example is the {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|qid=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|1=Ishinokarato Kofun}}Lower tier side length: 13.8 meters; upper tier diameter: 9.2 meters; total height: approximately 2.9 meters. The entire surface is covered with river stones, and the burial chamber is a horizontal stone cist. (also known as Kazahahi Kofun or Kazehai Kofun, located on the border of {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} and {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kizugawa, Kyoto|en|Kizugawa, Kyoto|qid=Q990352|lt=Kizugawa City|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}). It is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th or early 8th century, and it was excavated in 1979. The second confirmed example was discovered in 1985 at the foot of {{ill|Mount Ashitaka|en|Mount Ashitaka|qid=Q3247941|lt=Mount Ashitaka|1=Mount Ashitaka}} in {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Named {{ill|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb|ja|清水柳北1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb|qq=}}, it is estimated to have been built in the early 8th century.[http://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/kankou/rekishini/nrekishi/menu_1/shimizu.htm Numazu City Office, "Shimizu Ryuhoku No. 1 Kofun"] The third example, discovered in 2003, is the {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|qid=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|qid=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City (Tokyo)|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, dating to the mid to late 7th century.[http://www.city.fuchu.tokyo.jp/kurasu/bunka/maizo/kumanokohun/index.html Fuchū City Office, "National Historic Site: Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun"] The fourth example is the {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|qid=Q242885|lt=Mitaka City|1=Mitaka, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}. Although initially excavated in 1970, it was confirmed as a Jōen Kahōfun during a 2007 reassessment.[http://www.city.mitaka.tokyo.jp/a002/p003/g14/d00300769.html Observatory Site Kofun, Mitaka City] The fifth example is the {{ill|Nojikubo Kofun|ja|野地久保古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nojikubo Kofun|qq=}}, discovered in 2008 in {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|en|Shirakawa, Fukushima|qid=Q750587|lt=Shirakawa City|1=Shirakawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, and dating to the late 7th to early 8th century.[http://www.city.shirakawa.fukushima.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020001&WIT_oid=icityv2_001::Contents::1270 Shirakawa City Office, "History of Shirakawa"] The sixth example, confirmed in 2013, is the {{ill|Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|山王塚古墳|Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster=Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Sannōzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南大塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|en|Kawagoe, Saitama|qid=Q319740|lt=Kawagoe City|1=Kawagoe, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the largest confirmed Jōen Kahōfun to date, with a lower tier side length of 63 meters and a total height of 4.5 meters.[http://sgkohun.world.coocan.jp/archive/index.php/kawagoe_sannou2/ Saitama Ancient Tomb Archive] Another potential example is the {{ill|Miyazuka Kofun|ja|宮塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮塚古墳 (熊谷市)}} in {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya City|1=Kumagaya}}, Saitama Prefecture. Although the mound's measured dimensions suggest this classification, its exact shape remains uncertain due to a lack of excavation.Estimated to date from the late Kofun to early Heian periods. == Modern Jōen Kahōfun == This Kofun form has been adopted for modern imperial mausoleums, including the {{ill|Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum|ja|伏見桃山陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum|qq=}} of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}, the {{ill|Tama Mausoleum|ja|多摩陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Mausoleum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}}, and the {{ill|Musashi Imperial Graveyard|ja|武蔵野陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Imperial Graveyard|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武蔵陵墓地|1=Musashi Imperial Graveyard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6940153}} of Emperor {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Shōwa|1=Hirohito}}. The design was inspired by earlier examples such as the {{ill|Goryō-no-Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-no-Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and the {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}. Recent studies suggest these ancient tombs were actually {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} rather than true Jōen Kahōfun.Shiraishi Taichirō, "Jōen Kahōfun," in Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto (Eds.), ''Japanese Archaeology Encyclopedia,'' 2003, p. 394. == Broad Definition == Some scholars include octagonal tombs, which feature square lower tiers and octagonal upper tiers, in the Category of Jōen Kahōfun. The most famous example of this broader classification is the {{ill|Goryō-no-Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-no-Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of Emperor Tenji. There is also speculation that the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|qid=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} may have been constructed as a Jōen Kahōfun. == List of Examples == From north to south: * {{ill|Nojikubo Kofun|ja|野地久保古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nojikubo Kofun|qq=}} – Shirakawa City, Fukushima Prefecture (Designated a National Historic Site in 2010){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.shirakawa.fukushima.jp/sp/page/page001388.html |title=Shirakawa Funada and Honuma Archaeological Sites |publisher=Shirakawa City Cultural Heritage Division |accessdate=2017-11-11}} * {{ill|Miyazuka Kofun|en|Miyazuka Kofun|qid=Q11454036|lt=Miyazuka Kofun|1=Miyazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Minami-Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Minami-Otsuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Sannōzuka Kofun|ja|山王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Otsuka Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南大塚古墳群}} – Kawagoe City, Saitama Prefecture (City-designated Historic Site){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/hakkustsuchosa/sannoutsuka_3.html |title=Sannōzuka Kofun Third Excavation |publisher=Kawagoe City Board of Education |accessdate=2017-11-11}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} – Mitaka City, Tokyo Metropolis{{Cite web|url=http://www.mitaka-iseki.jp/tokubetsu3/kohun.pdf |format=PDF |title=Observatory Site Kofun |publisher=Mitaka City Board of Education |accessdate=2017-11-11}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|qid=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} – Fuchū City, Tokyo Metropolis (Designated a National Historic Site in 2005){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.fuchu.tokyo.jp/kirari/sagasu/theme/muryou/kumanoginzyakohun.html |title=National Historic Site: Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun |publisher=Fuchū City |accessdate=2017-11-11}} * {{ill|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb|ja|清水柳北1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb|qq=}} – Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture (Restored site){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm |title=Shimizu Ryuhoku No. 1 Kofun |publisher=Numazu City Board of Education |accessdate=2017-11-11}} * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|qid=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|1=Ishinokarato Kofun}} – Nara City, Nara Prefecture and Kizugawa City, Kyoto Prefecture (Designated a National Historic Site in 1996){{Kotobank|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|2=National Historic Sites Guide}} == See Also == * {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{nihongo|{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|八角墳|''hakkaku-fun''}} * {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} == References == == Further Reading == * Fuchū City and Kokubunji City Boards of Education, ''Excavating Ancient Musashi Provincial Temple and Capital Sites,'' Gakuseisha, 2006. ISBN 9784311202865. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto (Eds.), ''Japanese Archaeology Encyclopedia,'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == External Links == * [http://www.amy.hi-ho.ne.jp/mizuy/arc/kumanojinja/ Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|上円下方墳|version=103216201|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上円下方墳]] {{everybodywiki link|Joenkahofun}} [[en:Joenkahofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11358496}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Joenkahofun]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
